stories.title: Monaco : Aladji Bamba va signer en Angleterre pour plus de 40M€
stories.summary: Aladji Bamba, a 20-year-old midfielder, is set to join Newcastle in England for a transfer deal worth approximately 41 million euros, including a 5 million euro bonus, after reportedly being sold by AS Monaco. The deal marks Newcastle's fourth major signing this transfer season, with the club having already spent 95 million euros on other players. Bamba is expected to undergo a medical examination in England before finalizing the transfer.

stories.title: Reid Scott Leaving ‘Law & Order’, James Badge Dale To Succeed Him In Season 26 Of NBC Drama
stories.summary: Reid Scott is leaving the NBC drama "Law & Order" after three seasons, citing the strain of a cross-country commute and being away from his family for nine months of the year. He will be succeeded by James Badge Dale, who will play a new Senior Detective in Season 26. Scott's character, Det. Vincent Riley, will receive a proper send-off in upcoming episodes before Dale's introduction.

stories.title: World Earthquake Report for Thursday, 23 July 2026
stories.summary: A magnitude 5.1 earthquake struck the Central Mid-Atlantic Ridge on Thursday, July 23, 2026, at 03:29 am GMT-3, followed by four other earthquakes of magnitude 5.0 or higher in various locations around the world, resulting in a total seismic energy equivalent to one quake of magnitude 5.9. The earthquakes occurred in the past 24 hours, with a total of 499 reported quakes. The seismic activity is being closely monitored.

stories.title: AOC roasted over defense bill claim as GOP lawmaker tells her to 'stop stoking Jew hatred'
stories.summary: Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez has faced criticism for claiming the House-passed National Defense Authorization Act would merge parts of the US military with the Israel Defense Forces, a claim disputed by Republican lawmakers and officials who argue the legislation strengthens defense cooperation with Israel. The House passed its version of the FY2027 National Defense Authorization Act in a 219-206 vote, with Section 219 intact, which calls for deeper U.S.-Israel defense integration. Ocasio-Cortez's criticism has sparked a heated debate over the bill's provisions.

stories.title: VAT cut to be applied to Manx electricity bills
stories.summary: A 0% VAT rate will be applied to domestic electricity bills on the Isle of Man from October, mirroring the UK's measure in place until the end of the 2026-27 financial year. This change is expected to save a typical household around £50 per year, with the overall reduction in VAT revenue estimated to be between £1 million and £1.4 million. The Manx government has confirmed the move as part of efforts to ease cost-of-living pressures on households.

stories.title: Why are UK fuel prices rising again?
stories.summary: Fuel prices in the UK are rising again, with wholesale oil prices nearing $100 a barrel, following the collapse of peace talks to end the US-Israel war with Iran. This increase is expected to push up pump prices, with analysts estimating every $10 rise in oil price adds around 7p to the cost per litre. As a result, motorists can expect further price hikes in the coming days.

stories.title: UK millionaires ask Andy Burnham to tax them more
stories.summary: One hundred and twenty UK millionaires, including Gary Lineker and Brian Eno, have written to new Prime Minister Andy Burnham requesting a 2% tax on their wealth exceeding £10 million. This move is aimed at promoting a more equal society by redistributing wealth from the richest to the rest. The signatories believe this investment will benefit the country and help it succeed.

stories.title: St Helens boss Rowley leaves club day before derby
stories.summary: St Helens head coach Paul Rowley has stepped down from his position with immediate effect, leaving the club sixth in the table and six points behind leaders Leeds Rhinos. Rowley's departure comes after a difficult season, with the club struggling for form and losing six of their past 10 games in all competitions. Assistant coach Eamon O'Carroll will take over on an interim basis ahead of Friday's derby against Wigan Warriors.

stories.title: Newcastle agree £34m deal for Monaco's Bamba
stories.summary: Newcastle United have agreed a deal worth up to £34 million for Monaco midfielder Aladji Bamba, who will undergo a medical and sign a long-term contract on Thursday. The 20-year-old Frenchman is expected to strengthen the Magpies' midfield after the recent sale of Sandro Tonali to Tottenham. Bamba's impressive statistics in Ligue 1 last season, including 9.1 duels won per game, make him a valuable addition to the team.

stories.title: Venice Title ‘The Difficult Bride’ Taps Films Boutique for World Sales (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Bangladesh's first feature film to screen at the Venice Film Festival, "The Difficult Bride," has tapped Films Boutique for international sales. The supernatural feminist drama, directed by Rubaiyat Hossain, will compete in the Horizons section of the festival. It marks a significant milestone for Bangladeshi cinema, with three national award winners among its cast.

stories.title: Isabel Davis Named Director of BFI Filmmaking Fund – Global Bulletin
stories.summary: Isabel Davis has been appointed director of the BFI National Lottery Filmmaking Funds, overseeing £20 million annually in support for U.K. independent filmmakers. She begins a five-year contract in October, succeeding Mia Bays who has overseen several BAFTA wins since 2021. Davis joins the BFI from Screen Scotland, where she served as executive director for eight years.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, a 23-year-old who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The move would see Liverpool beat Arsenal and Bayern Munich to the signature of the French forward. Barcola's potential transfer is now set to be a major focus for the Reds as they prepare for the upcoming season.

stories.title: River levels approach all-time low as drought announcement looms
stories.summary: River levels in Wales are approaching all-time lows, with the country seeing just 5% of its average July rainfall due to a series of heatwaves and a dry spring. This has led to significant problems for rivers, killing fish and wildlife, and also affecting farming with failing crops and heat-stressed livestock. An announcement from Natural Resources Wales is expected later on Thursday, which is likely to confirm an environmental drought in parts of the country.

stories.title: Tom Holland Is Filming Fred Astaire Biopic Next and Plans to ‘Do All of the Dancing’ Himself: ‘I’ve Got So Much Work to Do’
stories.summary: Tom Holland is set to star in a biopic about Fred Astaire, with filming reportedly underway. The actor plans to perform all of his own dance scenes, a nod to Astaire's own approach to filming dance numbers. Holland's preparation for the role has already begun, with rehearsals taking place in the dance studio.

stories.title: Framework’s premium laptop is shipping with less RAM
stories.summary: Framework's premium Laptop 13 Pro is shipping with less RAM than initially promised, affecting customers who preordered the device. The company has adjusted memory configurations due to a significant price increase from its memory supplier, which is now more than double the original cost. As a result, Framework is offering cancellations with full deposit refunds to customers who are unhappy with their new configuration.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark deal allowing the Gulf kingdom to develop a civilian nuclear program, described as a "peaceful nuclear co-operation agreement" that could provide access for American companies in the Saudi nuclear energy program. The deal includes a bilateral safeguards agreement and a co-operation deal, laying the foundation for a decades-long, multi-billion-dollar partnership. This marks a significant development in the region, with experts warning it could run the risk of future nuclear proliferation in an already unstable area.

stories.title: Thousands evacuated in France fires
stories.summary: Thousands of people have been evacuated from the south-west of France due to ongoing wildfires, with over 12,000 residents relocated and more than 2,000 hectares of land destroyed. The use of internal combustion and electric engines has been banned in predominantly forested areas, and several roads have been closed. Firefighting efforts are ongoing, with no reported injuries so far.

stories.title: A Russian Spy, Suddenly Cast Into the Spotlight, Flees Japan
stories.summary: The military intelligence officer left the country after The Times reported that he was using the Aeroflot airline office in Tokyo to conduct espionage.

stories.title: Diesel Crisis Puts Poor Farmers Around the World at Risk
stories.summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has limited the shipment of diesel, depriving farmers of fuel for irrigation pumps and threatening the food supply.

stories.title: A New Target for Ukraine’s Long-Range Attacks: Russia’s Version of Amazon
stories.summary: Kyiv is escalating its air war by striking commercial warehouses, trying to bring the conflict closer to home for ordinary Russians.

stories.title: Oil-Rich Saudi Arabia Isn’t Short on Energy. Here’s Why It’s Going Nuclear.
stories.summary: The world’s largest exporter of crude oil stands to benefit from diversifying its energy sources — and freeing up more oil to sell.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet has reported its first negative free cash flow in at least a decade, coming in at $5.9 billion, due to spiralling costs of artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company's spending on AI is expected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion, as major tech companies invest heavily in the technology. This significant outlay has led to a 4% drop in Alphabet's stock price in after-hours trading.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

stories.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
stories.summary: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
stories.summary: Indian activist Lobsang Wangchuk has been on a hunger strike for 26 days, losing 11 kilograms, as he protests against repeated exam paper leaks in the country. Prime Minister Narendra Modi has responded to the protests by announcing the establishment of fast-track courts to punish those involved in the leaks. Wangchuk's demands remain unmet, with the activist's supporters vowing to continue their struggle until their concerns are addressed.

stories.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
stories.summary: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

stories.title: How to Use ChatGPT and Gemini Prompts to Find Out What They Know About You
stories.summary: It can be unsettling what Gemini and ChatGPT have figured out about you and how easily your privacy can be punctured. Here’s how to find out.

stories.title: Butterfly city blooms in shadow of office blocks
stories.summary: In Hull city centre, a "butterfly city" has been created with the discovery of ten species of butterfly in a "mini meadow" near St Stephen's Shopping Centre. The meadow, established in 2023, features grass and wild flowers that provide food for caterpillar species, including the small skipper butterfly, which breeds in the area. This rare urban sighting is attributed to the meadow's unique environment, where grass is left uncut throughout the year to support the caterpillar's winter habitat.

stories.title: Alarm over hospital lung admissions for children in England’s poorest postcodes
stories.summary: Children living in England's poorest postcodes are 40% more likely to be admitted to hospital for respiratory infections, with 1,941 out of every 100,000 children in the most deprived areas being admitted in 2024/25. This contrasts with 1,380 admissions per 100,000 in the least deprived areas, resulting in a total of 82% more hospital admissions for respiratory infections in the poorest areas. Experts are now raising concerns about the impact of air pollution on young people's health in these areas.

stories.title: Life-changing MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
stories.summary: A life-changing multiple sclerosis treatment, fampridine, is now available on the NHS in England, benefiting up to 5,000 people annually. This "signal booster" drug helps nerve signals, allowing some individuals to move and live independently. The decision is expected to make a significant difference in the lives of those with multiple sclerosis, enabling them to walk and engage in daily activities.
